{{Quote|Capital City is where you'll find the most people on this world. It's where people run the businesses of Lothal.|Ezra Bridger|<ref name="A New Hero">''[[Star Wars Rebels: A New Hero]]''</ref>}}
'''Capital City''', also known as '''Lothal City''', was the main [[City|population center]] and [[capital]] of [[Lothal]], a [[planet]] in economic disrepair that had invited the [[Galactic Empire]] in return for prosperity and security. During the [[Imperial Era]], Capital City became the center of Imperial power on Lothal and housed the [[Imperial Complex (Lothal)|Imperial Command Center]] and the [[Academy for Young Imperials]]. Despite the Imperial presence, various rebel and criminal groups such as the [[Spectres]] rebel cell, the [[Lothal resistance group]], and the [[Gray Syndicate]] also operated in Capital City.

==Description==
===Architecture and infrastructure===
[[File:Imperial_complex.png|thumb|left|250px|The Imperial Complex]]
Founded by Lothal's original colonists, Capital City was established on the site of the planet's primary trading outpost. It was a simple<ref name="NoP" /> yet modern<ref name="VE">''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]''</ref> city, resembling many other trading hubs on the fringes of the galaxy, consisting of single and two–story prefabricated buildings. At the heart of the city was the [[Lothal City Capitol Building]], where the planetary government assembled to govern and administer to the economy of the agriworld. Expanding outward, rather than upward, new structures were built with local stone and simple materials like [[plasteel]] that was imported.<ref name="NoP" />

During the [[Imperial Era]], many of these simple structures were demolished to make way for Imperial–style [[skyscraper]]s. About half of the city was also depopulated to make way for Imperial industrial and military buildings, resulting in a mixture of architecture types.<ref name="NoP" /> Some notable Imperial facilities in Capital City included the [[Imperial Complex (Lothal)|Imperial Complex]] and the [[Academy for Young Imperials]].<ref name="SWRTVG">''[[Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide]]''</ref> Capital City also hosted several marketplaces.<ref name="Spark of Rebellion">''[[Star Wars Rebels: Spark of Rebellion]]''</ref> The Imperial Complex towered over all the other buildings in Capital City and turned out to be a mobile [[Imperial Planetary Occupation Facility]].<ref name="Family Reunion">{{Rebels|Family Reunion – and Farewell}}</ref>

Some notable educational institutions in Capital City included the [[Junior Academy of Applied Sciences]] ("AppSci"), the [[Vocational School for Institutional Security]], the [[Technical Institute for Agricultural Research]],<ref name="EotG" /> and the [[Phelarion School]].<ref name="RitR">''[[Servants of the Empire: Rebel in the Ranks]]''</ref> The town's outskirts hosted the [[Ake's Tavern]], later used by the criminal cartel known as the [[Gray Syndicate]], while its industrial quarter had a number of old warehouses, such as [[1044 Chapel]].<ref name="TSA" /> In addition, Capital City hosted a [[Capital City spaceport|spaceport]] with docking facilities and a [[posting agency]] that allowed [[bounty hunter]]s to access the [[Imperial Enforcement DataCore]].<ref name="Ezra's Gamble" />

Capital City also had a [[Capital City zoo|zoo]]<ref name="EDWD" /> and a [[Lothal City fuel depot|fuel depot]].<ref name="Rebel Assault" /> Capital City was also the site of the [[Imperial Armory Complex]], which manufactured various vehicles and weapons for the [[Imperial Military]]. These included [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter|TIE fighters]], [[All Terrain Defense Pod|AT-DP walkers]], [[All Terrain Armored Transport (Prototype)|AT-AT walkers]], [[speeder bike]]s, and the prototype [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter]] initiative.<ref name="AIM">{{Rebels|An Inside Man}}</ref>

[[File:Capital_City_spaceport_shuttle.png|thumb|right|250px|Capital City's spaceport]]

===Inhabitants===
Capital City was home to a wide range of species including [[human]]s, [[Gotal]]s, [[Ithorian]]s,<ref name="Spark of Rebellion" /> [[Rodian]]s, [[Aqualish]],<ref name="EotG" /> [[Ugnaught]]s, and [[Faust]]s.<ref name="Imperial Justice">''[[Servants of the Empire: Imperial Justice]]''</ref> Some human residents such as [[athletic director]] [[Janus Fhurek]] expressed [[Xenophobia|xenophobic views]] towards aliens and discriminated against them. [[Grav-ball]] was also a popular sport among Capital City's young people.<ref name="EotG" />

===Law enforcement and defense===
[[Imperial officer]]s and [[stormtrooper]]s were responsible for enforcing law and order in Capital City. Besides criminals, Imperial forces also had to contend with rebel groups such as the [[Spectres]].<ref name="Spark of Rebellion" /> While firearms were banned in civilian zones of the city, this was flouted by bounty hunters such as the [[Trandoshan]] [[Bossk'wassak'Cradossk|Bossk]]. The Imperial authorities allowed members of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild]] to pursue Imperial fugitives throughout Lothal including Capital City.<ref name="Ezra's Gamble" />

The Imperial Military was responsible for Capital City's defenses. In addition to the Imperial fleet,<ref name="Rebel Assault" /> the city was defended by nine gunnery stations of four anti-air [[turbolaser]] towers each.<ref name="Rebel Assault Trivia Gallery">{{SW|url=series/star-wars-rebels/rebel-assault-trivia-gallery|text="Rebel Assault" Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 5}}</ref> One of these gunnery stations was destroyed during the [[Attack on Lothal]] in [[1 BBY]] when all four of its turbolaser towers were destroyed by the Spectres and the [[Lothal resistance group]].<ref name="Rebel Assault" />

==History==
===Origins===
[[File:Rebels_at_Lothal_Senate_Building.png|thumb|left|250px|Lothal City Capitol Building]]
Capital City was founded by [[Lothal]]'s first colonists on the site of the planet's primary trading outpost.<ref name="NoP" /> During the time of the [[Galactic Republic]], Capital City was the site of the Lothal City Capitol Building, which was the seat of Lothal's government.<ref name="Vision of Hope" />

===Imperial rule===
====Imperialization====
After the [[Clone Wars]], the [[Galactic Empire]] responded to pleas from the beleaguered planetary government and established a permanent presence in Capital City. Using eminent domain laws, the Imperial [[Governor]] seized and demolished property in the city's center to pave the way for Imperial style skyscrapers. Depopulating entire sectors of the city to make way for industrial and military buildings, the Empire employed mass arrests and violent crackdowns to supplant local insurrections and maintain the peace. Imperial patrols maintained a strict curfew, and abuse of the locals was frequent and violent.<ref name="NoP" />

Following the removal of Governor [[Ryder Azadi]], his successor Governor [[Arihnda Pryce]] oversaw the expansion of Imperial industrial, mining, and military projects and facilities on Lothal including Capital City.<ref name="Thrawn">[[Thrawn (novel)|''Thrawn'']]</ref> In response to Lothal's economic development, several [[Core Worlds]] families including the [[Leonis family]] and the [[Spanjaf family]] settled in Capital City. Many farmers whose land had been purchased by the Empire for mining projects such as [[Beck Ollet]]'s family also settled in Capital City. While many people welcomed the prosperity and stability brought by the [[New Order]], some like Beck resented the loss of land and the Empire's unwillingness to address peoples' grievances.<ref name="EotG" />

After his parents [[Ephraim Bridger|Ephraim]] and [[Mira Bridger]] were arrested by the Imperial authorities for speaking out against the Empire, their son [[Ezra Bridger]] lived on the streets of Capital City for several years.<ref name="Legacy">{{Rebels|Legacy (episode)}}</ref> He eked out a living as a street urchin, pick pocket, and con artist under the guidance of the [[Xexto]] pawnshop owner [[Ferpil Wallaway]]. Bridger assisted Bossk in hunting the [[Dug]] criminal [[Gronson Takkaro]] and subsequently exposing the corrupt [[Imperial Security Bureau]] officer [[Lieutenant]] [[Jenkes]].<ref name="Ezra's Gamble" />

====Rise of the Rebellion====
[[File:Officers_Harass_Merchant.png|thumb|right|250px|Imperial officers and stormtroopers patrolling Capital City's marketplace]]
By [[5 BBY]], the Spectres rebel cell had developed a presence in Capital City; harrying the local Imperial authorities. The [[Lasat]] rebel [[Garazeb Orrelios]] was once embroiled in a brief skirmish with Imperial stormtroopers and a [[Imperial Starfighter Pilot|TIE fighter pilot]] after coming to the aid of an [[Unidentified Ugnaught salesman|Ugnaught salesman]] and his droid.<ref name="Entanglement">{{Rebels|Entanglement}}</ref> Later, Ezra was caught up in the Spectres' heist to steal Imperial supplies in Capital City's markets. Following a [[Mission to free Wookiee prisoners|brief mission]] to free [[Wookiee]] prisoners on [[Kessel]], Ezra decided to join the Spectres after [[Kanan Jarrus]] offered to train the [[Force-sensitive]] boy in the ways of the [[Jedi]].<ref name="Spark of Rebellion" />

Later, Ezra infiltrated the Imperial Academy to obtain information about a [[kyber crystal]] shipment offworld. He was helped by the [[Imperial cadet]] [[Zare Leonis]], who was searching for his sister [[Dhara Leonis]] who had been kidnapped by [[the Grand Inquisitor]].<ref name="Breaking Ranks">{{Rebels|Breaking Ranks}}</ref> Zare was also aided by his girlfriend [[Merei Spanjaf]], who used [[snooper]] programs to [[Transportation Ministry data breach|hack]] into the Imperial database at the [[Transportation Ministry]]. Merei found out that Dhara had been inducted into a secret Imperial project called [[Project Harvester]], that was based on the planet [[Arkanis]]. Merei's data breach triggered an Imperial investigation led by her mother [[Jessa Spanjaf]], who was a leading Imperial data security investigator.<ref name="RitR" />

In [[4 BBY]], the Spectres disrupted the [[Empire Day]] celebrations in Capital City by blowing up a prototype [[TIE/ad v1]] starfighter, humiliating [[Minister]] [[Maketh Tua]], [[Commandant]] [[Cumberlayne Aresko]], and [[Taskmaster]] [[Myles Grint]]. In addition, the rebels evacuated the Rodian [[Tseebo]], a former [[Imperial Information Office]] employee who had deserted the Empire.<ref name="Empire Day" /> In response, Governor Pryce launched a mass security crackdown on Lothal including Capital City. This led to the arrests of many criminals and dissidents. Under the direction of [[Captain]] [[Piers Roddance]], Imperial cadets at the local Imperial Academy including Zare Leonis and [[Nazhros Oleg]] were also deployed as auxiliaries during the crackdown.<ref name="Imperial Justice" />

Minister Tua and the ISB [[Agent]] [[Alexsandr Kallus|Kallus]] also tried to capture the Spectres by using the faux dissident [[Senator]] [[Gall Trayvis]] to lure the rebels into a [[Trap in the Old Republic Senate Building|trap]] in the Lothal City Capitol Building. However, the Spectres escaped and Trayvis was exposed as an Imperial agent.<ref name="Vision of Hope" /> Meanwhile, Merei found herself indebted to Gray Syndicate [[crime lord]] [[Yahenna Laxo]], who had supplied her with the snoopers. Laxo forced her to do several courier jobs until Merei managed to break free of the Gray Syndicate by framing Laxo for the Transportation Ministry data breach and her own kidnapping. As a result, Imperial stormtroopers [[Assault on the Gray Syndicate|stormed]] the Gray Syndicate's headquarters and wiped out the crime syndicate.<ref name="Imperial Justice" />

In response, [[Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin]], the Governor of the Outer Rim, visited Capital City to chastise the local Imperial authorities for their failure. He had the [[Grand Inquisitor]] execute Aresko and Grint as a warning to Minister Tua and Agent Kallus. Under Tarkin's direction, Kallus and the Grand Inquisitor managed to capture Kanan in the nearby town of [[Jalath]] but failed to apprehend the other Spectres.<ref name="CTA">{{Rebels|Call to Action}}</ref> The destruction of the [[Imperial Communications Center]] disrupted communications throughout Lothal including Capital City. As a result, many industries were forced to rely on [[courier droid]]s for transporting information.<ref name="TSA" />

Prior to [[Action at Mustafar|rescuing]] Jarrus, the Spectres stole a [[Gozanti-class cruiser|''Gozanti''-class cruiser]] from an Imperial airfield. In response to Kanan's escape, the [[Darth Sidious|Emperor]] dispatched [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] to deal with the rebel presence on Lothal.<ref name="FATG">{{Rebels|Fire Across the Galaxy}}</ref> After her friend [[Jix Hekyl]] was arrested over his role in the Transportation Ministry data breach, Merei, her parents, and the Leonis parents fled offworld with the help of the Ithorian bartender [[Jho|Old Jho]] and the Spectres rebel cell.<ref name="TSA" />

====Imperial lockdown====
[[File:Lothal_Imperial_factory_assembly_lines.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Imperial Armory Complex on Lothal]]
Under the leadership of Darth Vader, the Empire launched a renewed search for the Spectres rebel cell. This led to a [[Siege of Lothal|harsh crackdown]] on Lothal. When Minister Tua attempted to defect from the Empire, the Spectres traveled to Capital City to rescue her. Before they could escape, Tua was killed by Agent Kallus in a bomb that was blamed on the Spectres in an effort to turn Lothal against the insurgents. The rebels hid in the former Bridger household but were pursued by Imperial forces. The Spectres then infiltrated the Imperial Command Center only to be ambushed by Vader. Following a fierce duel, the Spectres managed to escape Capital City on a [[Sentinel-class landing craft|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]].<ref name="TSOL" />

Later, Ezra, Kanan, and the [[C1-series astromech droid]] [[C1-10P|Chopper]] landed in Capital City during a visit to find the former prisoner Ryder Azadi, who knew Ezra's parents. Azadi revealed that Ezra's parents had perished during an [[Revolt in an Imperial prison|escape]] from an Imperial prison.<ref name="Legacy" /> Azadi subsequently became the leader of another rebel cell on Lothal, which included operatives like Old Jho, the former farmer [[Morad Sumar]], and his wife [[Marida Sumar]]. Azadi's group specialized in sabotaging vehicles produced by Capital City's Imperial Armory Complex, resulting in a high rate of defective Imperial equipment produced on Lothal.<ref name="AIM" />

By [[2 BBY]], [[Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo|Thrawn]], a rising star in the [[Imperial Navy]], decided to take action against the saboteurs. He ordered Morad Sumar to ride a defective speeder bike, which claimed the life of the rebel operative. Kanan, Ezra, and Chopper subsequently infiltrated the factory and obtained intelligence on the Empire's TIE/D Defender initiative. They managed to escape with the help of Agent Kallus, who had become the secret rebel operative known as [[Fulcrum]]. Thrawn soon realized that the Empire had a mole within their ranks.<ref name="AIM" />

Later, Thrawn briefed Governor Pryce and [[Admiral]] [[Kassius Konstantine]] about the rogue Senator [[Mon Mothma]]'s escape at the Imperial Command Center. Pryce and Konstantine led a [[Operation Handoff|failed attempt]] to apprehend the rogue senator.<ref name="Secret Cargo">{{Rebels|Secret Cargo}}</ref> Prior to the [[Battle of Atollon]], Thrawn held a secret meeting with Pryce and Konstantine to discuss intelligence about the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance's]] plan to attack the Imperial Armory Complex on Lothal. This meeting was a ruse to lure Agent Kallus into revealing his identity as Fulcrum and the location of [[Phoenix Cell]]'s base.<ref name="Zero Hour">{{Rebels|Zero Hour}}</ref>

In 1 BBY, the Rebel Alliance launched an air raid against the Imperial Armory Complex in Capital City under the leadership of [[Hera Syndulla]]. Despite Hera's Spectre comrades and their local allies taking out the Imperial artillery defense, Grand Admiral Thrawn's fleet and TIE fighters shot down most of the rebel strike force. Several rebel starfighters crashed-landed in the streets of Capital City. While [[Secon Daree]] and Hera were captured by the [[Noghri]] tracker [[Rukh]], Chopper and [[Mart Mattin]] managed to flee into the sewers and rendezvous with the other rebels. Kanan also encountered a mythical [[loth-wolf]] while driving through Capital City's highway.<ref name="Rebel Assault" />

Governor Pryce then [[torture]]d Hera for information about the [[Rebel Alliance Navy|Alliance Fleet]] at [[Pryce's office]] at the Imperial Complex on Lothal. Hera remained defiant during interrogation. Later, Kanan, Ezra and Sabine used [[Loth-bat glider]]s to infiltrate the Imperial Complex. While Kanan rescued Hera, Ezra and Sabine stole a [[LAAT/le patrol gunship|patrol transport]]. They rendezvous at the Lothal City fuel depot only to be pursued by Governor Pryce and several AT-AT walkers. Determined to destroy the rebels, Pryce ordered the AT-AT walkers to open fire on the fuel depot. Kanan was killed during the explosion but held back the explosive wave long enough for his rebel comrades to escape on their patrol transport.<ref name="Jedi Night">{{Rebels|Jedi Night}}</ref> To cover up the destruction of the fuel depot and the disruption of TIE Defender production on Lothal, Governor Pryce convened a parade where all citizens and workers were compelled to attend. However, Grand Admiral Thrawn along with Sabine and Zeb saw through the facade and realized that the Empire had lost.<ref name="DUME">{{Rebels|DUME}}</ref>

===Liberation of Lothal===
While Pryce was supervising the Imperial search for rebels at the Imperial Complex, she was contacted by Ryder. Ryder pretended to betray his fellow rebels in return for escaping imprisonment. However, this was part of a plot to lure Pryce and her forces into a trap at the [[Lothal cliff dwelling]], which served as the Lothal resistance and Spectres' new base on Lothal. The plan worked and Pryce was captured.<ref name="A Fool's Hope">{{Rebels|A Fool's Hope}}</ref> Using the captive Pryce, the rebels infiltrated the Imperial Complex. As part of their plan to [[Liberation of Lothal|liberate]] Lothal from Imperial rule, Ryder initiated [[Protocol 13]] to evacuate all Imperial forces on Lothal.<ref name="Family Reunion" />

Before they could fire the Imperial Complex's thrusters, Thrawn returned aboard the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Chimaera]]''. Thrawn managed to force Ezra to meet him aboard the ''Chimaera'' by bombarding Capital City, causing considerable damage and killing and injuring many civilians. While Ezra parleyed with Thrawn and the Emperor, the other rebels managed to reactivate the two [[deflector shield generator]]s before Thrawn unleashed his second bombardment wave. The rebels were then reinforced by a pod of space-faring [[purrgil]], which wiped out Thrawn's [[Seventh Fleet (Galactic Empire)|Seventh Fleet]] and carried Thrawn and Ezra aboard the ''Chimaera'' into the unknown reaches of space.<ref name="Family Reunion" />

Completing their mission, the rebels then fired the Imperial Complex's thrusters and took the mobile occupation facility into Lothal's upper atmosphere. They then evacuated aboard the ''Ghost'' before detonating the Complex. Pryce, who elected to stay behind, and most of the Imperial garrison on Lothal were killed when the Imperial Complex exploded in orbit. Many of Capital City's civilians regarded this as the end of Imperial rule on Lothal and cheered. Several Imperial stragglers including an officer and stormtroopers were also taken prisoner by the locals.<ref name="Family Reunion" />

===Beyond the Empire===
[[File:SWR_Capital_City_Post_Endor.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Capital City after the fall of the Empire]]
Following the destruction of the "Dome" and the end of Imperial rule, Lothal's cityscape returned to its original white hue. Despite fears that the Empire would return, the Empire never returned to Lothal and collapsed following the [[Battle of Endor]].<ref name="Family Reunion" /> Following the final defeat of Imperial forces at the [[Battle of Jakku]], the [[New Republic]] became the dominant government of the galaxy and ushered in a new order.<ref name="TFAVD">''[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> Sabine took up residence in Ezra's old roost and painted a [[Spectres mural|mural]] of her fellow Spectres with loth-wolves for the Capital City. At some time following the events on [[Endor]], Sabine and the former Jedi Padawan [[Ahsoka Tano]] embarked on a [[Quest to find Ezra Bridger|quest]] to bring their friend and comrade Ezra Bridger home.<ref name="Family Reunion" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Heist_on_Lothal_Concept_Art.png|thumb|right|250px|Concept art of Capital City]]
Lothal's Capital City first appeared in [[Michael Kogge]]'s book ''[[Rise of the Rebels]]'', which was launched as part of the release of the [[Star Wars Rebels Season One|first season]] of the animated [[Disney XD]] series ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' in Fall [[2014]].
